title: Confidentiality Agreement with Auto-Renewal and Perpetual Survival

issues.rationale: Perpetual confidentiality survival creates an indefinite obligation that may be unenforceable in many jurisdictions. Combined with auto-renewal (90-day notice buried), this creates a permanent confidentiality obligation. The prompt's CRITICAL rule for 'confidentiality terminates completely' does not apply, but perpetual survival is still a MEDIUM-HIGH enforceability risk.

ground_truth.rationale: Two issues combined: (1) confidentiality survives perpetually (indefinite), and (2) auto-renewal with 90-day notice period buries the termination mechanism. The perpetual survival is the primary risk. CRITICAL severity would apply if confidentiality 'terminates completely' — here it survives perpetually, which is different.
